previously_unidentified_papers.title: Compartmentalized cytokine networks and systemic immune remodeling in bovine mammary H5N1 infection
previously_unidentified_papers.authors: Gagandeep Singh et al., Kansas State University
previously_unidentified_papers.annotation: Intramammary H5N1 B3.13 challenge in dairy cows; compartment-resolved cytokine/chemokine profiling, peripheral leukocyte dynamics, IFN-gamma-competent lymphocyte activation. Immunological rather than molecular-mechanistic; no specific host-pathogen protein interactions at single-molecule level. Complements existing H5N1 dairy cluster (nasal epithelium, neuroinvasion, mRNA vaccine, natural immunity papers).
previously_unidentified_papers.abstract_snippet: HPAI H5N1 genotype B3.13 emerged in US dairy cattle with pronounced mammary tropism. Intramammary challenge in Jersey cows; dose- and compartment-resolved cytokine and chemokine profiles with peripheral leukocyte dynamics and H5/N1-specific antibody responses.

previously_unidentified_papers.title: Pathogen-pathogen interactions between influenza and RSV
previously_unidentified_papers.annotation: Epidemiological study of pathogen-pathogen interactions between respiratory viruses including influenza and RSV. Not focused on molecular host-pathogen interactions.

previously_unidentified_papers.title: Vaccine-induced antigenic drift of a human-origin H3N2 Influenza A virus in swine alters glycan binding and sialic acid avidity
previously_unidentified_papers.annotation: Antigenic drift characterization of H3N2 in swine with glycan binding changes. Structural/evolutionary focus rather than host-pathogen molecular interaction at cellular level.

previously_unidentified_papers.title: Natural H5N1 immunity in dairy cows is durable and cross-protective but non-sterilizing
previously_unidentified_papers.annotation: Characterization of natural immunity following H5N1 infection in dairy cows - durable, cross-protective, non-sterilizing. Immunological profiling, not molecular interaction discovery. Adds to H5N1 dairy cluster.

notes: Fifth scan of 2026-03-11. No new influenza HPMI papers found within the 3-day window (March 8-11). Four previously unidentified papers found outside the window, none scoring >= 0.6. The H5N1 dairy/zoonotic research cluster continues to grow (bovine mammary cytokine paper from March 6 adds immunological depth) but these papers focus on immunological profiling rather than molecular host-pathogen interactions at the protein level. Publication rate for influenza HPMI papers on bioRxiv remains low for this period, consistent with scan d observations.
